title: lathyrus laevigatus subsp. krylovii
intro: Lathyrus laevigatus subsp. krylovii, also known as Krylov's pea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of arthritis, and acting as a natural remedy for kidney stones. In herbal preparations, Lathyrus laevigatus subsp. krylovii is often used to make infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or added to bath waters for therapeutic purposes.
